what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML test page that loads shared surface styles/runtime scripts and renders a gallery of artefact-related organism visuals by injecting DNA.org output into captioned cells.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Inline CSS creates a padded warm page, flex-wrapped gallery row, captioned cells, borders, rounded corners, and shadow styling.
Loads /surface/runtime/surface.js and /surface/runtime/organisms.js before calling DNA.org helpers.
Builds cell markup with a small JavaScript helper and assigns a concatenated HTML string to the row container.

Renders multiple parameter variations for designLibraryObject, fileStack, planThumbnail, projectTileGrid, versionStack, sourcePanelVisual, evidenceBand, interfaceFragment, artifactFlow, and artifactCluster.
Includes both synthetic examples and examples using real asset paths under /review/ingest/deck1-2026/.
standouts: The file is a compact visual specimen board for many artefact organism variants.
Several examples intentionally vary density, size, highlight index, current version, count, and real asset input.
Captions document the tested configuration beside each rendered organism.
conventions: Uses absolute project-root paths for shared CSS, runtime scripts, and review assets.
Keeps the specimen data inline rather than loading fixtures from a separate structure.
Mixes static HTML structure with inline styling and inline JavaScript for a single-purpose test page.
Uses string concatenation and innerHTML to render generated organism markup.
concerns: The page assumes global DNA.org helpers exist after the runtime scripts load.
The absolute asset and runtime paths make the file dependent on a specific serving root.
Inline JavaScript and inline styles make the specimen page less reusable outside this file.
Generated SVG/HTML strings are inserted with innerHTML, relying on trusted local inputs.
notes: A local artefact-organism gallery page for visually checking generated component variants.
questions: Should the real asset paths be treated as required fixtures or optional visual examples?
Is this page intended for manual inspection only, or also for automated visual regression capture?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and heading describe an artefact organism test page, and the file does render a gallery of artefact-family organism examples, though it covers more organism types than the heading's designLibraryObject emphasis.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make artefact-related organism outputs quickly inspectable across common parameter combinations and asset modes.
novelty: A dense, file-local specimen board that combines generated synthetic organism states with real image-backed examples in one page.
contribution: Provides a concrete visual test ledger for artefact organism renderings, including parameterized variants, captions, and real-asset fixture cases that a fused system could keep as inspection coverage.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML specimen page that renders captioned DNA.org artefact organism visuals with multiple parameter variants and real asset examples.
intention_for_embedding: Created to visually exercise and inspect artefact-family organism components in a compact test gallery.
